2606.22314.title: Diffusion Integrated Gradients: Controllable Path Generation for Flexible Feature Attribution
2606.22314.paper_summary: Path-based attribution methods such as Integrated Gradients (IG) are widely adopted for their strong axiomatic properties and effectiveness in attributing model predictions to input features by integrating gradients along a path from a baseline to the input. However, the choice of the attribution path largely affects the quality of explanations, and existing approaches rely on fixed or hand-crafted paths that often produce noisy or distorted attributions. To address this limitation, we propose Diffusion Integrated Gradients (DiffIG), a novel method that reformulates path generation as a conditional generative modeling problem. DiffIG first trains a diffusion model to learn a distribution over paths generated from a Stick-Breaking Process, then employs guided sampling to embed user guidance during the sampling procedure. We demonstrate that DiffIG quantitatively matches or outperforms existing path-based methods, achieving perceptually aligned explanations. This work introduces a new generative perspective for flexible, inference-time controllable Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) methods.
2606.22314.paper_summary_zh: 基於路徑的歸因方法，如整合梯度（Integrated Gradients, IG），因其強大的公理性質和有效地將模型預測歸因於輸入特徵的能力而被廣泛採用，通過沿著從基線到輸入的路徑整合梯度。然而，歸因路徑的選擇在很大程度上影響解釋的質量，而現有的方法依賴於固定或手工製作的路徑，這些路徑往往產生噪音或失真的歸因。為了解決這一限制，我們提出了擴散整合梯度（Diffusion Integrated Gradients, DiffIG），這是一種將路徑生成重新表述為條件生成建模問題的新方法。DiffIG 首先訓練一個擴散模型，以學習從 Stick-Breaking Process 生成的路徑的分佈，然後在採樣過程中使用引導採樣來嵌入用戶指導。我們證明了 DiffIG 在量化上匹配或超越現有的基於路徑的方法，實現了感知上對齊的解釋。本研究為靈活的、推理時可控的可解釋人工智慧（Explainable Artificial Intelligence, XAI）方法引入了一種新的生成視角。
2606.22314.author: Soyeon Kim et.al.
2606.22314.authors: Soyeon Kim, Kyowoon Lee, Jaesik Choi
